reviews.review_msg: I've had Rahman for a few classes and I think he's a great professor. Easy going and if you show a genuine interest in his subject material he will want you to succeed. Has a lot of reading required, so prepare for that. Overall he tries his best to make the class interesting so if you show face that is what matters.

I encourage people to take Dr. Rahman's class; his lectures are like "edutainment" and he's just generally a pleasant person to be in the same room with. HW is light. He's also a very snappy dresser, so you're not giving him a makeover in your mind while he's trying to lecture (not sure if I'm the only one who gets distracted by that thought).
Anis is a generous and respected person! He could be strict at times but overall a very good person and CMNS444 is a very good class overal!

He showed the Raid and John Wick movie action scenes in class, then step by step analyzed how the Indonesian Pensak Silat martial art has become globalized through films. Amazing! Also, learned that Wonder Woman was co-produced with a Chinese film giant that also owns majority share of AMC theatres in the US. Wow!

Anis changed my view of Facebook, Apple and Google forever. I'll never look at Apple products same way after taking this course. Readings are hard and often boring but eye-opening lectures and friendly tutorials. Must attend lectures if u want a grade above B in exams

One of the most caring professors at SFU. He's very knowledgeable about international affairs. I've taken 3 courses with him and always enjoyed informative lectures and got rewarded for my participation in class. He seemed to struggle with assignment guideline in this course, perhaps bcz he tried to incorporate too many suggestions from students

he's good enough to take a course with. yes we can go with him and even take him again. but get ready to read . loads of readings to be done. and probably the longest essay you will ever write will be for this course.

Brutal lectures. He overloads students with WAY more examples than needed, way past the point when everyone understands the concept. Not good with technology, wastes a lot of time trying to get videos to load. Assignment guidelines unclear and disorganized.
Sometimes he mumbled and jump from one theory to another randomly. It becomes hard and difficult to follow. Unclear outline of the class.

Gives a lot of examples which makes some of the dry stuff more interesting. Genuinely wants his students to do good and is very specific about what is required from this course. Study for exams but have a lot of examples on hand to show your understanding and look out for the questions in readings and slides as he slips them in there.
